title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
text: Sharing individual-level clinical data across institutions often restricted privacy regulations institutional review boards. Synthetic data preserves statistical properties real data reducing re-identification risk, enabling multi-site collaboration without data transfer.

title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
code: library(syntheticdata) set.seed(42) real <- data.frame(   age     = rnorm(500, mean = 65, sd = 12),   sbp     = rnorm(500, mean = 135, sd = 22),   sex     = sample(c("Male", "Female"), 500, replace = TRUE),   smoking = sample(c("Never", "Former", "Current"), 500,                    replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.35, 0.25)),   outcome = rbinom(500, 1, 0.28) ) head(real) #>        age      sbp    sex smoking outcome #> 1 81.45150 157.6411   Male   Never       0 #> 2 58.22362 155.1250 Female Current       1 #> 3 69.35754 134.9460   Male   Never       0 #> 4 72.59435 137.9922   Male  Former       0 #> 5 69.85122 119.1566 Female Current       0 #> 6 63.72651 130.6413 Female  Former       0

title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
text: default method estimates marginal distributions empirically captures joint dependence structure via Gaussian copula normal scores. preserves marginal shapes pairwise correlations.
code: syn <- synthesize(real, method = "parametric", n = 500, seed = 1) syn #>  #> ── Synthetic data (parametric) #> 500 real -> 500 synthetic records #> Variables: age, sbp, sex, smoking, outcome

title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
text: validate_synthetic() computes four classes metrics: KS statistic: distributional similarity (lower better). Correlation difference: preservation variable associations. Discriminative AUC: can classifier distinguish real synthetic? Values near 0.5 mean indistinguishable. NN distance ratio: privacy metric. Values 1 indicate synthetic records memorizing real individuals.
code: val <- validate_synthetic(syn) val #>  #> ── Synthetic data validation #> ks_statistic_mean: 0.0247 (Good fidelity) #> correlation_diff: 0.0141 (Excellent) #> discriminative_auc: 0.5117 (Indistinguishable) #> nn_distance_ratio: 0.8595 (Moderate risk)

title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
text: compare_methods() runs three synthesis methods data returns single comparison table:
code: comp <- compare_methods(real, seed = 1) comp #>  #> ── Synthesis method comparison #> # A tibble: 12 × 4 #>    method     metric              value interpretation    #>  * <chr>      <chr>               <dbl> <chr>             #>  1 parametric ks_statistic_mean  0.0247 Good fidelity     #>  2 parametric correlation_diff   0.0141 Excellent         #>  3 parametric discriminative_auc 0.512  Indistinguishable #>  4 parametric nn_distance_ratio  0.988  Moderate risk     #>  5 bootstrap  ks_statistic_mean  0.142  Acceptable        #>  6 bootstrap  correlation_diff   0.0181 Excellent         #>  7 bootstrap  discriminative_auc 0.505  Indistinguishable #>  8 bootstrap  nn_distance_ratio  1.09   Good privacy      #>  9 noise      ks_statistic_mean  0.135  Acceptable        #> 10 noise      correlation_diff   0.0162 Excellent         #> 11 noise      discriminative_auc 0.501  Indistinguishable #> 12 noise      nn_distance_ratio  1.36   Good privacy

title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
text: privacy_risk() provides deeper privacy audit three metrics: nearest-neighbor distance ratio, membership inference accuracy, (optionally) attribute disclosure risk sensitive columns.
code: pr <- privacy_risk(syn, sensitive_cols = "age") pr #>  #> ── Privacy risk assessment #> [!] nn_distance_ratio: 1.0682 (Medium) #> [OK] membership_inference_acc: 0.497 (Low) #> [OK] attribute_disclosure_age: 2e-04 (Low)

title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
text: model_fidelity() trains predictive model synthetic data evaluates real data. real-data baseline uses -sample evaluation upper bound. synthetic-trained model AUC close real-trained baseline indicates synthetic data preserves predictive signal.
code: mf <- model_fidelity(syn, outcome = "outcome") mf #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   train_data metric value #>   <chr>      <chr>  <dbl> #> 1 real       auc    0.523 #> 2 synthetic  auc    0.502

title: Generating and validating synthetic clinical data
text: Higher noise_level improves privacy reduces utility:
code: results <- list() for (nl in c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5)) {   s <- synthesize(real, method = "noise", noise_level = nl, seed = 1)   v <- validate_synthetic(s)   results <- c(results, list(data.frame(     noise_level = nl,     ks = v$value[v$metric == "ks_statistic_mean"],     privacy = v$value[v$metric == "nn_distance_ratio"]   ))) } do.call(rbind, results) #>   noise_level        ks   privacy #> 1        0.05 0.1373333 0.7011986 #> 2        0.10 0.1346667 1.3123723 #> 3        0.20 0.1393333 1.9435324 #> 4        0.50 0.1673333 4.6841489

what: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
title: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
text: Clinical researchers face familiar impasse. Multi-site studies require pooling patient records across institutions, yet privacy regulations — GDPR Europe, HIPAA United States — impose stringent barriers sharing identifiable health data. Ethics boards demand anonymisation; data use agreements take months negotiate; resulting delays can stall research precisely moment timely evidence matters . Synthetic data offers principled way forward. Rather de-identifying real records (can reversed certain conditions), generate entirely new observations reproduce statistical structure original dataset without retaining individual’s information. challenge, course, demonstrating synthetic data useful enough support downstream analysis private enough satisfy regulators. syntheticdata R package, now available CRAN, built address concerns within single workflow. provides generation, validation, privacy auditing five core functions — deep learning infrastructure required, Python bridges, GPU dependencies.

title: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
text: Three synthesis engines cover different points privacy–utility spectrum: Parametric synthesis fits Gaussian copula rank-transformed observations, preserving marginal distributions full correlation structure. produces genuinely new records — resampled rows — tends yield best utility metrics. Bootstrap synthesis resamples replacement injects calibrated Gaussian noise, suitable data distributional assumptions unwelcome. Noise-based synthesis applies Laplace perturbation (mechanism underpinning differential privacy) settings privacy requirements dominate utility concerns. interface deliberately minimal: returned object bundles synthetic original data, enabling every subsequent validation step without juggling separate data frames.
code: library(syntheticdata)  # Generate synthetic version of a clinical cohort syn <- synthesize(clinical_data, method = "parametric", n = 500, seed = 42)

title: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
text: synthetic dataset good evidence supporting fidelity safety. validate_synthetic() computes four complementary metrics single call: Kolmogorov–Smirnov statistic (distributional fidelity) — mean KS across numeric variables; values 0.1 indicate close marginal agreement. Correlation difference — Frobenius norm real synthetic correlation matrices; 0.05 excellent. Discriminative AUC — logistic classifier attempts distinguish real synthetic records. AUC near 0.5 means classifier tell apart. Nearest-neighbour distance ratio — median ratio synthetic--real versus real--real nearest-neighbour distances. Values 1.0 indicate synthetic records memorising real individuals. deeper privacy auditing, privacy_risk() adds membership inference accuracy attribute disclosure risk user-specified sensitive columns:
code: val <- validate_synthetic(syn) val pr <- privacy_risk(syn, sensitive_cols = c("age", "diagnosis_code"))

title: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
text: Consider hypothetical multi-site readmission study. site holds patient-level data demographics, vitals, binary readmission outcome. Site wishes share data Site B collaborative model development, export identifiable records. model_fidelity() function trains predictive model synthetic records, evaluates held-real data, compares performance real-data baseline. AUC R-squared values close, synthetic data preserves enough signal intended analytical purpose. optimal synthesis strategy unclear, compare_methods() benchmarks three engines dataset returns side--side comparison table — practical shortcut method selection.
code: library(syntheticdata)  # Site A generates synthetic data syn <- synthesize(site_a_data, method = "parametric", n = 1000, seed = 1)  # Validate utility validate_synthetic(syn)  # Audit privacy privacy_risk(syn, sensitive_cols = "age")  # Test whether a model trained on synthetic data # performs comparably to one trained on real data model_fidelity(syn, outcome = "readmitted", predictors = c("age", "sbp", "comorbidity_count"))

what: Where This Fits in the Ecosystem
title: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
text: Several R packages address synthetic data generation. synthpop (Nowok, Raab, Dibben 2016) targets survey census data sequential modelling. simstudy (Goldfeld 2020) simulates user-specified distributions trial design. simPop focuses population-level microsimulation. syntheticdata occupies different niche. starts observed clinical data (user-specified parameters), couples generation privacy assessment, includes downstream model fidelity testing. teams need demonstrate IRB data protection officer synthetic records statistically adequate re-identification-resistant, integrated workflow eliminates need stitch together separate tools.

title: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
text: Gaussian copula approach works well datasets moderate dimensionality mix continuous categorical variables — common profile clinical research. high-dimensional data (thousands features), complex temporal structures, generative adversarial networks sequential synthesis methods may appropriate. package attempt replace approaches; provides lightweight, dependency-minimal alternative datasets commonly encountered clinical epidemiological work. Privacy metrics interpreted context. nearest-neighbour distance ratio 1.0 reassuring constitute formal privacy guarantee differential privacy sense. Laplace noise synthesis moves closer standard, though calibrating noise parameter ε requires domain-specific judgment acceptable utility loss.

title: Privacy Without Paralysis: Generating Synthetic Clinical Data in R
text: package ships single vignette walking full generation-validation-auditing pipeline. Source code issue tracking available GitHub. Feedback clinical data teams working GDPR, HIPAA, institutional privacy constraints particularly welcome — privacy metrics interpretive thresholds benefit validation across diverse real-world settings. Cuiwei Gao health data analyst R developer. syntheticdata package available CRAN.
code: install.packages("syntheticdata")

what: Synthetic Clinical Data Generation and Privacy-Preserving Validation
title: Synthetic Clinical Data Generation and Privacy-Preserving Validation
text: Synthetic Clinical Data Generation Privacy-Utility Validation

title: Synthetic Clinical Data Generation and Privacy-Preserving Validation
text: syntheticdata generates synthetic clinical datasets preserve statistical properties reducing re-identification risk. Useful privacy-aware data sharing multi-site clinical research. Generation: Gaussian copula, bootstrap noise, Laplace noise perturbation Validation: distributional fidelity (KS), correlation preservation, discriminative accuracy Privacy assessment: nearest-neighbor distance ratio, membership inference, attribute disclosure risk Benchmarking: compare_methods() runs methods data; model_fidelity() measures train--synthetic, test--real predictive performance Unlike synthpop (survey data) simPop (census microsimulation), syntheticdata integrates generation privacy-utility validation single lightweight framework oriented toward clinical research.  Figure 1 | Synthetic data preserves statistical properties ensuring privacy. Fisher’s iris dataset (n = 150, 4 numeric variables) synthesized via Gaussian copula. () Marginal density overlays: synthetic (orange) closely matches real (blue) across variables (mean KS = 0.06). (b) Pairwise correlation preservation (Frobenius diff = 0.028). (c) Validation metrics: discriminative AUC = 0.53 (indistinguishable random), nearest-neighbor distance ratio = 1.73 (privacy leakage). Data: Fisher (1936) Ann. Eugenics 7:179.

title: Synthetic Clinical Data Generation and Privacy-Preserving Validation
text: gap: CRAN package combines generation + privacy assessment + downstream model fidelity testing one workflow. Existing tools either generate without validating, validate without privacy-aware metrics.
code: # Complete workflow in 3 lines syn <- synthesize(clinical_data, method = "parametric") privacy_risk(syn, sensitive_cols = c("diagnosis", "age")) model_fidelity(syn, outcome = "readmission")

title: Downstream model fidelity test — model_fidelity
text: Trains predictive model synthetic data evaluates real data. Compares model trained real data (gold standard). Measures whether synthetic data preserves predictive signal.

title: Downstream model fidelity test — model_fidelity
code: model_fidelity(x, outcome, predictors = NULL)

title: Downstream model fidelity test — model_fidelity
text: x synthetic_data object. outcome Character. Name outcome column. predictors Character vector (optional). Predictor columns. Default: numeric columns.

title: Downstream model fidelity test — model_fidelity
text: tibble columns: train_data, metric, value. binary outcomes metric AUC; continuous outcomes R-squared.

title: Downstream model fidelity test — model_fidelity
text: real-data baseline uses -sample evaluation (train test real data) provide upper bound achievable performance. synthetic-data model also evaluated real data, comparison reflects well synthetic data preserves predictive signal.

title: Downstream model fidelity test — model_fidelity
text: Jordon J, et al. (2022). Synthetic Data – , ? arXiv preprint arXiv:2205.03257. doi:10.48550/arXiv.2205.03257

title: Downstream model fidelity test — model_fidelity
code: set.seed(42) real <- data.frame(     x1 = rnorm(200), x2 = rnorm(200),     y = rbinom(200, 1, 0.3)) syn <- synthesize(real, seed = 42) model_fidelity(syn, outcome = "y") #> Warning: non-integer #successes in a binomial glm! #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   train_data metric value #>   <chr>      <chr>  <dbl> #> 1 real       auc    0.577 #> 2 synthetic  auc    0.438

what: Generate synthetic data from a real dataset — synthesize
title: Generate synthetic data from a real dataset — synthesize
text: Creates synthetic version input data preserves marginal distributions pairwise correlations adding controlled noise privacy protection.

title: Generate synthetic data from a real dataset — synthesize
code: synthesize(   data,   method = c("parametric", "bootstrap", "noise"),   n = nrow(data),   noise_level = 0.1,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate synthetic data from a real dataset — synthesize
text: data data frame real clinical data. method Synthesis method: "parametric" (default): fits Gaussian copula continuous variables, multinomial categorical. Fast, interpretable. "bootstrap": nonparametric resampling optional noise. "noise": adds calibrated Laplace noise variable (differential privacy inspired). n Number synthetic records. Default: input. noise_level method = "noise": scale Laplace noise relative variable SD. Default 0.1. seed Random seed reproducibility. non-NULL, global RNG state saved restored synthesis calling code affected.

title: Generate synthetic data from a real dataset — synthesize
text: synthetic_data object (list) components: $synthetic (tibble synthetic records), $real (tibble original data, retained downstream validation), $method, $n_original, $n_synthetic, $variables.

title: Generate synthetic data from a real dataset — synthesize
text: parametric method uses Gaussian copula approach: marginal distributions estimated empirically joint dependence structure captured via correlation matrix normal scores. preserves marginal shapes pairwise associations generating genuinely new observations.

title: Generate synthetic data from a real dataset — synthesize
text: Jordon J, et al. (2022). Synthetic Data – , ? arXiv preprint arXiv:2205.03257. doi:10.48550/arXiv.2205.03257

what: syntheticdata: Synthetic Clinical Data Generation and Privacy-Preserving Validation — syntheticdata-package
title: syntheticdata: Synthetic Clinical Data Generation and Privacy-Preserving Validation — syntheticdata-package
text: Generates synthetic clinical datasets preserve statistical properties reducing re-identification risk. Implements Gaussian copula resampling, bootstrap noise injection, Laplace noise perturbation, built-utility privacy validation metrics.

text: Maintainer: Cuiwei Gao 48gaocuiwei@gmail.com [copyright holder]

title: Validate synthetic data quality — validate_synthetic
text: Computes utility privacy metrics comparing synthetic data original real dataset.

title: Validate synthetic data quality — validate_synthetic
code: validate_synthetic(   x,   metrics = c("distributional", "correlation", "discriminative", "privacy") )

title: Validate synthetic data quality — validate_synthetic
text: x synthetic_data object synthesize(). metrics Character vector metrics: "distributional": KS statistic per numeric variable. "correlation": Frobenius norm correlation difference. "discriminative": AUC real-vs-synthetic classifier. "privacy": nearest-neighbor distance ratio.

title: Validate synthetic data quality — validate_synthetic
text: synthetic_validation object (tibble) columns: metric, value, interpretation.

title: Validate synthetic data quality — validate_synthetic
text: Utility metrics assess well synthetic data preserves statistical properties. Privacy metrics assess risk re-identification. Discriminative accuracy near 0.5 means synthetic data indistinguishable real data. Privacy ratio > 1 means synthetic records closer real records real records .

title: Validate synthetic data quality — validate_synthetic
text: Snoke J, et al. (2018). General specific utility measures synthetic data. Journal Royal Statistical Society , 181(3):663–688. doi:10.1111/rssa.12358

title: Validate synthetic data quality — validate_synthetic
code: set.seed(42) real <- data.frame(age = rnorm(100, 65, 10), sbp = rnorm(100, 130, 20)) syn <- synthesize(real, seed = 42) validate_synthetic(syn) #>  #> ── Synthetic data validation  #> ks_statistic_mean: 0.085 (Good fidelity) #> correlation_diff: 0.0263 (Excellent) #> discriminative_auc: 0.5319 (Indistinguishable) #> nn_distance_ratio: 0.8892 (Moderate risk)

title: Twitter/X Announcement Thread — syntheticdata
text: Tweet 1: New CRAN: syntheticdata — R package generating synthetic clinical datasets built-privacy auditing. Gaussian copula, bootstrap, Laplace noise engines. Five functions, minimal dependencies, GPU required. #rstats #SyntheticData #OpenSource Tweet 2: problem: multi-site clinical studies need data sharing, HIPAA/GDPR make slow painful. Synthetic data preserves statistical structure without retaining individual’s information. syntheticdata validates utility re-identification risk workflow. Tweet 3: Generation → validation → privacy audit → model testing. Four lines. #HealthData #PrivacyPreserving Tweet 4: sets apart: CRAN package integrates synthesis + KS/AUC validation + membership inference + attribute disclosure risk + downstream model fidelity one pipeline. Built teams need show IRB synthetic records safe share. Tweet 5: Install: install.packages(“syntheticdata”) CRAN: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=syntheticdata Feedback welcome, especially clinical data teams navigating GDPR/HIPAA constraints. — @CuiweiG23 #ClinicalResearch #DataPrivacy #OpenSource
code: syn <- synthesize(clinical_data, method = "parametric") validate_synthetic(syn) privacy_risk(syn, sensitive_cols = "diagnosis") model_fidelity(syn, outcome = "readmitted")

title: syntheticdata 0.1.1
text: Initial release. synthesize(): Parametric (Gaussian copula), bootstrap noise injection, Laplace noise perturbation methods. validate_synthetic(): Distributional similarity (KS), correlation preservation, discriminative accuracy (AUC), nearest-neighbor privacy ratio. compare_methods(): Benchmark three synthesis methods dataset. privacy_risk(): Re-identification risk assessment via nearest-neighbor distance ratio, membership inference, attribute disclosure. model_fidelity(): Train--synthetic, test--real predictive performance comparison.
